mnemonics.title: Elementary Row Operations
mnemonics.content: SWAP-MULT-ADD: Remember S-Swap rows, M-Multiply row by non-zero, A-Add multiple of one row to another. These are reversible!

mnemonics.title: System Types
mnemonics.content: CONsistent: COuld be None (no solution) - wait, that's wrong. COnsistent: COuld be Unique or Many. INConsistent: INfinite (no, wait) - INConsistent has No solution.

mnemonics.title: RREF Rules
mnemonics.content: Leading 1s go Right-Down; Each 1 is alone in its Column; All Zero rows at Bottom.

acronyms.usage: Matrix form where leading entries cascade from top-left to bottom-right
REF plus each leading 1 is the only non-zero in its column

acronyms.expansion: Not an acronym - named after Gauss
acronyms.usage: Method to solve linear systems by forward elimination then back-substitution

acronyms.expansion: Not an acronym - named after Gauss and Jordan
acronyms.usage: Extension of Gaussian that goes all the way to RREF

visualTips.visualization: Think of two lines: intersecting lines meet at one point (consistent), parallel lines never meet (inconsistent), same line overlaps completely (consistent-dependent)
Use the rank equation: If rank(A) = rank([A|B]) = n (variables), unique solution. If rank = rank < n, infinite. If ranks differ, no solution.
Visualize a staircase descending from top-left: each step (leading entry) is to the right and below the previous one, with zeros below each step.
Always passes through origin (0,0) - think of lines/planes always containing origin, so at least the trivial solution exists.

analogies.analogy: Like reorganizing books on a shelf - you can swap positions, multiply contents, or add one book's content to another. The information (solution) stays the same.
Like having two recipes that say 'add 1 cup of sugar' - one is redundant, gives no new information.
Pivots are like cornerstones of a building - they define the structure and support other elements.
Like a receipt showing both items bought (coefficients) and total price (constants) together in one place.
